authors_affiliation: Zheheng Jiang, Tiago P Almeida, Fernando S Schlindwein, G Andre Ng, Huiyu Zhou, Xin Li (University of Leicester, Leicester, UK)

result.evaluation_split: official hidden full test set (abstract: validation score 0.395, full test score -0.012, rank 34/41)

self_reported_metrics.other: Internal 5-fold cross-validation ablation (Table 1), not the hidden test set: 1DCNN Challenge Metric 0.503 / F1 0.481; +Res-Blocks 0.554 / 0.526; +Bi-GRU 0.582 / 0.564; +GCN + class-aware BCE (final model) 0.627 / 0.603. Official Challenge validation-phase score reported separately as 0.395.

model.architecture_details: ECG representation network: 1D convolutional layer + 16 residual blocks (2 conv layers each, filter width 16, filters starting at 32 and doubling every 4th block, stride-2 subsampling every 2nd block, BatchNorm + ReLU + dropout 0.2) followed by a bi-directional GRU layer to extract temporal features; a 3-layer Graph Convolutional Network (GCN) then models inter-label correlations, using a correlation matrix built from label co-occurrence conditional probabilities; final classification via dot product of ECG features and GCN label embeddings, trained with a proposed class-aware binary cross-entropy loss.
model.key_novelty: Combines a CNN+bi-GRU ECG feature extractor with a Graph Convolutional Network that explicitly models label co-occurrence/correlation structure (via a conditional-probability adjacency matrix) for multi-label classification, trained jointly with a novel class-aware BCE loss.

input.signal_length_window: variable length; recordings shorter than 18 seconds were zero-padded; network outputs one prediction every 512 samples
input.sampling_rate: 500 Hz (all recordings resampled to this rate)
input.preprocessing: Resampling to 500 Hz; zero-padding of recordings shorter than 18s

data.training_datasets: Official PhysioNet/CinC 2020 Challenge datasets: China Physiological Signal Challenge 2018 (10330 recordings), St. Petersburg INCART 12-lead Arrhythmia Database (75 recordings), PTB (Physikalisch Technische Bundesanstalt, 22386 recordings), and Georgia 12-Lead ECG Challenge Database (10344 recordings); 25 classes used (C=25)

training.loss_function: Proposed class-aware binary cross-entropy loss (incorporates GCN label-embedding dot product with ECG features), compared against standard BCE in ablations
training.optimizer: Adam (stochastic gradient descent variant), learning rate 0.001, reduced by factor of 10 when validation loss plateaus for 2 consecutive epochs; batch size 32; 100 epochs
training.cross_validation_strategy: 5-fold cross-validation on the training set for ablation experiments

contribution.summary: Proposes an end-to-end multi-label ECG classifier that pairs a CNN+bi-GRU feature extractor with a Graph Convolutional Network encoding label co-occurrence correlations, trained via a new class-aware BCE loss, showing incremental gains from residual blocks, bi-GRU, and GCN in ablation.
